layout.title: Bike Trip Planner
layout.description: Plan your bikepacking trips with ease

stage.travelTimeTooltip: Estimate based on average speed and elevation gain (Naismith rule adapted for cycling)

restDay.label: Rest day

pacing.coherenceWarning: Warning: your average speed is very low for the maximum daily distance set.
pacing.maxDistanceTooltip: Hard cap on daily distance (km), applied after the pacing formula.
pacing.averageSpeedTooltip: Average cycling speed (km/h), used for travel time estimation.
pacing.fatigueTooltip: Reduces distance by {value}% per day to compensate for accumulated fatigue.
pacing.elevationTooltip: Reduces distance by {value}% per 100m of positive elevation gain.
pacing.ebikeModeTooltip: Enable range alerts for e-bike stages (80 km base range, adjusted for elevation).
pacing.departureHourTooltip: Typical departure hour, used for travel time estimates on each stage.

config.title: Settings
config.splitViewTooltip: The map is always visible next to the timeline. This toggle will allow manually hiding/showing the map.

textExport.budgetNote: Estimated budget per stage: accommodation (selected or average of suggestions, excluding last stage) + ~12–20€/meal × 3 meals/day — except the first stage (no breakfast) and the last (no dinner).

map.elevationProfileAriaLabel: Interactive elevation profile for the trip

onboarding.step1Description: Copy a link from Komoot (tour or collection) and paste it here. Your trip will be computed automatically.
onboarding.step2Description: Already have a GPX file? Click this button to import it directly and plan your trip from existing route data.
onboarding.step3Description: Open Settings to adjust your pacing, fatigue factor, elevation penalty, and accommodation preferences before computing your stages.
onboarding.step4Description: Once your trip is loaded, stages appear in the timeline. Each stage shows distance, elevation, travel time, alerts, and accommodation suggestions. You can reorder, split, or add rest days as needed.

hero.title: Your co-pilot for every kilometre
hero.subtitle: Plan your cycling trip with complete peace of mind. Route analysis, terrain alerts, weather, accommodation and resupply — all in one place.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1Description: Paste a Komoot, Strava, RideWithGPS link or upload a GPX file.
howItWorks.step2Description: Map and timeline display your automatically computed stages.
howItWorks.step3Description: Our engine detects terrain alerts, weather, accommodation and resupply points.
howItWorks.step4Description: Download your optimised GPX/FIT and depart with peace of mind.

terrain.title: Terrain & Safety
terrain.description: Alerts for border crossings, train stations, nearby emergency services.

supply.title: Resupply
supply.description: Water points, grocery shops, restaurants detected along your route.

ai.title: AI Assistant
ai.description: Generate or optimise your itinerary with artificial intelligence. Import from a conversation, route variant suggestions.

accommodation.title: Accommodation
accommodation.description: Campsites, gîtes, hotels suggested at each stage with estimated budget.

weather.title: Weather
weather.description: 7-day weather forecasts for each stage of your trip.

services.title: Services
services.description: Pharmacies, doctors, bike repair shops flagged in case of emergency.

availability.title: Available everywhere
availability.responsiveDescription: Optimised for all screen sizes, from smartphone to widescreen.
availability.pwaDescription: Install the app on your device like a native application.
availability.androidDescription: Native Android version for an optimal mobile experience.
availability.offlineDescription: Browse your trips without a connection during your journey.

1.text: An indispensable tool for planning my bikepacking trips. The terrain alerts saved me from several nasty surprises!
1.author: Marie L.

2.text: The automatic stage management and Komoot integration are mind-blowing. I can't imagine planning without this tool anymore.
2.author: Thomas B.

3.text: The AI assistant is really useful for generating route variants. The app is still in beta but already very comprehensive.
3.author: Sophie M.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about Bike Trip Planner.
faq.q1: What sets Bike Trip Planner apart from Komoot or RideWithGPS?
faq.a1: Komoot and RideWithGPS are GPS navigation platforms. Bike Trip Planner is a multi-day itinerary planning tool: it automatically splits your route into daily stages based on your cyclist profile (speed, accumulated fatigue, elevation), suggests accommodations at each stage endpoint, and generates contextual alerts (weather, resupply, health services, etc.). It complements your navigation apps rather than replacing them.
faq.q2: Why use this tool instead of planning manually on a map?
faq.a2: Manual planning is time-consuming and often underestimates accumulated fatigue over multiple days. Bike Trip Planner applies an automatic pacing formula that progressively reduces daily distance to account for fatigue, incorporates elevation into the calculation, and suggests accommodations directly at each stage endpoint — all within seconds.
faq.q3: Is the project independent of platforms like Komoot or Strava?
faq.a3: Yes. Bike Trip Planner is an independent project. It uses GPX exports or shared links from Komoot, Strava, and RideWithGPS only to retrieve route data, but does not depend on any commercial partnership with those platforms.
faq.q4: What data is stored on the server?
faq.a4: Your trip configuration (dates, profile, stages) is stored in a database so you can access it from any device. Raw route data (GPS points) is cached temporarily in Redis and deleted automatically. No personal data other than your email address is collected.
faq.a5: Bike Trip Planner accepts Komoot tour and collection links, Strava routes, and RideWithGPS routes. You can also import a GPX file directly from your computer.
faq.a6: Partially. In offline mode, you can view previously loaded trips in read-only mode. Creating or editing a trip requires an internet connection, as computations (pacing, alerts, accommodation suggestions) are performed server-side.
faq.q7: Can I use the app on mobile?
faq.a7: Yes. The app is a responsive PWA (Progressive Web App) optimised for mobile and tablet. You can install it on your home screen from your mobile browser for quick access, without going through an app store.
faq.q8: How do I get access to the app?
faq.a8: The app uses a magic link sign-in system: enter your email address on the sign-in page and you will receive a secure sign-in link. No password is required.
faq.q9: Is the app free and open source?
faq.a9: The source code is open source and available on GitHub. The hosted app is free to use. Infrastructure costs may eventually justify a freemium model, but no paid access is planned at this stage.
